data.title: Radian gets double upgrade to Buy at BofA as Inigo buy brightens outlook
data.description:
Radian Group (RDN) stock gained 3.7% in late morning trading on Wednesday after Bank of America Securities upgraded the stock to Buy from Underperform based on Radian's transformation into a global, diversified specialty insurer from a U.S. mortgage insurer. In February, the company completed its acquisition of specialty insurance group Inigo for $1.7B.

"RDN is redeploying capital from a low-return, cyclical real estate services platform into a higher-ROE, uncorrelated specialty insurance earnings stream," analyst Mihir Bhatia wrote in a note to clients. "Inigo is a profitable, diversified reinsurer operating through Lloyd's and is guided to double revenue over time and be mid-teens EPS accretive."

BofA raised its price target on Radian (RDN) to $43 from $35. "We see a clear path to a re-rating as capital efficiency improves and the market prices Inigo’s contribution," Bhatia said.

The analyst acknowledges the risk Radian is taking on with its acquisition outside of mortgage insurance but sees its retention of Inigo's team, brand, and underwriting autonomy as mitigating factors.

The Buy rating contrasts with the SA Quant rating and average Wall Street rating of Buy.

data.title: AI adoption is splitting along income lines
data.description:
Artificial intelligence (AIQ) (AIEQ) use in the workplace is emerging as a high-income advantage, according to data from the Federal Reserve Bank of New York.

The survey found that 66.3% of workers earning more than $200K had used AI tools in their jobs over the past 12 months as of November 2025, according to data recently shown by Arbor Research. That compares with just 15.9% of workers earning less than $50K.

AI adoption rose almost step by step with income: 40.2% for workers earning $50K to $100K, 51.6% for those earning $100K to $200K and nearly two-thirds for the highest earners.

More recent adoption measures point in a similar K-shaped direction. Claude maker Anthropic's (ANTHRO) Economic Index found AI usage remains concentrated and strongly correlated with income across countries, suggesting the early AI productivity dividend is still clustering around higher-income workers and environments.

data.title: Uber adds hotel bookings as part of its goal to be the app for everything
data.description:
Uber Technologies (UBER) announced a new set of products and features at its annual GO–GET product event. The most notable reveal was that Uber (UBER) will now take hotel bookings. 

Through a partnership with Expedia Group (EXPE), Uber (UBER) customers in the U.S. will be able to book hotels directly in the Uber app. The wide selection of hotels is anticipated to ultimately grow to more than 700K properties in destinations around the globe. In addition, vacation rentals from Expedia (EXPE) Group brand, Vrbo, will be added later this year.

Following an initial pilot, Uber (UBER) rides will be integrated directly in the Expedia app (EXPE) beginning in June. Travelers will receive push notifications before their hotel check-in date to book Uber rides discounted for the duration of their trip. The partnership is expected to expand beyond the U.S.

The update was noted to show Uber's (UBER) continued platform strategy to bring more of everyday life into one simple user experience. "Uber is becoming an app for everything—helping people go, get, and now travel all in one place," noted Uber (UBER) CEO Dara Khosrowshahi.

data.title: Premium Brands gains as activist investor sees potential 75% upside
data.description:
Premium Brands (PBH:CA) rose 1.5% as an activist investor suggested the shares could see 75% upside from current levels.

Alta Fox, which has a 1.5% stake in Premium Brands (PBH:CA), argues that the company's shares are "materially undervalued."

"The stock now trades at decade-low valuation multiples more reflective of ex-growth, structurally challenged peers despite Premium Brands’ consistent organic volume growth, which has accelerated to double digits in the last two quarters," Alta Fox said in a statement  on Wednesday.

Alta Fox wants Premium Brands to be clearer with messaging around its 2027 earnings power. Specifically, the company should provide an updated FY2027 EBITDA target and commit to a defined FY2027 free cash flow target. 

Premium Brands (PBH:CA) didn't immediately return Seeking Alpha's request for comment.

Premium Brands (PBH:CA) is set to report Q1 results next Thursday.

data.title: Element Solutions projects $665M-$685M 2026 adjusted EBITDA while lifting CapEx to $75M-$100M
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Element Solutions (ESI) Q1 2026

Management View

  • "Element Solutions started 2026 strong. We reported a record quarter yesterday that demonstrates ongoing success with our strategy of penetrating the highest value, fastest-growing subsegments in our addressable markets." (CEO & Director Benjamin Gliklich)
  • "Sales in our Electronics segment grew 15% organically as activity accelerated across our supply chain in support of the ongoing AI infrastructure build-out." (CEO & Director Gliklich)
  • "Forecasts from customers are increasing and innovation cycles are accelerating." (CEO & Director Gliklich)
  • "Organic net sales grew 10%, and constant currency adjusted EBITDA increased 21% year-over-year." (President of Enterprise Operations & CFO Carey Dorman)
  • "Pass-through metals revenue was $256 million in the first quarter of 2026 and $101 million in the fourth quarter of 2025." (CFO Dorman)

Outlook

  • "We're raising our adjusted EBITDA guidance to a range of $665 million to $685 million for the full year." (CEO & Director Gliklich)
  • "We expect second quarter adjusted EBITDA in the range of $155 million to $170 million, with demand conditions sequentially similar to the first quarter and taking into consideration some risk from raw material and logistics inflation that we may not recapture immediately despite ongoing sourcing and pricing actions." (CEO & Director Gliklich)
  • "We now expect 2026 adjusted EPS growth in the high teens on a full year basis." (CEO & Director Gliklich)
  • Compared with the prior quarter, management moved from "Our 2026 adjusted EBITDA guidance range is $650 million to $670 million" to "$665 million to $685 million" and added that the 2026 range "contemplates a less favorable FX tailwind than we expected a few months ago." (CEO & Director Gliklich)

Financial Results

  • "On this new basis, adjusted EBITDA margin improved 170 bps year-over-year to 27.8% this quarter." (CFO Dorman)
  • "Adjusted EPS grew 21% in the first quarter, largely reflecting the underlying demand improvement in our Electronics business and offset by higher interest costs associated with our recent acquisition activities." (CFO Dorman)
  • "Micromax, which we own for 2 months of this quarter, is not included in our organic net sales growth calculation, but contributed roughly $65 million to reported sales in the quarter." (CFO Dorman)
  • "Free cash flow was negative." (CFO Dorman)
  • "CapEx in the quarter was $25 million" and "we now expect to invest between $75 million and $100 million in CapEx this year." (CFO Dorman)

Q&A

  • Bhavesh Lodaya, BMO Capital Markets Equity Research: Within Electronics, how much of the 15% organic growth was volume vs. pricing/mix, and how to think about continuation? CEO Gliklich: "most of our growth in the first quarter has been volume driven" and "our outlook is for a continued robust volume environment."
  • Bhavesh Lodaya, BMO Capital Markets Equity Research: Where is stepped-up CapEx going, and what about plant consolidation? CEO Gliklich: "Kuprion is something that we're investing heavily in" and "in our Industrial business, we've been reducing our footprint" including "a large site consolidation project in one site in Europe."
  • Joshua Spector, UBS Investment Bank, Research Division: What is driving higher confidence in the guide, and is there better demand visibility? CEO Gliklich: "we've got confidence in the durability of this acceleration in demand through 2026 from data center and associated investment" and noted Micromax "seasonality and lumpiness" as a factor in phasing.
  • Joshua Spector, UBS Investment Bank, Research Division: Is Micromax contributing more than initially expected? CEO Gliklich: "it's a stronger result from an organic perspective" and "we are expecting better contribution from Micromax on a full year basis" while "still working" on seasonality.
  • Christopher Parkinson, Wolfe Research, LLC: Any changed expectations across Electronics and what’s improved? CEO Gliklich: "we do believe that PCB growth will outstrip what was originally forecast" and "the smartphone market... is better for us than forecast."
  • Christopher Parkinson, Wolfe Research, LLC: Kuprion commercialization trajectory? CEO Gliklich: "Commercialization is good" and "the note of caution I'd strike is just supply chain and our ability to meet demand."
  • Michael Harrison, Seaport Research Partners: Is metal volatility disrupting demand; threat or opportunity; and any changes to hedging/pass-through approach? CEO Gliklich: "higher metal prices have not had an impact on demand as yet" and cited competitors "running into cash flow issues" as a benefit; on hedging, "we're comfortable with the practice and don't expect anything to change."
  • John Ezekiel Roberts, Mizuho Securities USA LLC, Research Division: What was industry growth in Electronics and any updated thoughts on memory impacts? CEO Gliklich: "The data isn't out yet" and "our organic results are outstripping the overall industry"; on memory, "it accelerates the transition of our business away from consumer electronics and towards enterprise applications."
  • Rock Hoffman Blasko, BofA Securities, Research Division: Is weaker smartphone shipment outlook in guidance, and timing/scale of pricing actions? CEO Gliklich: "our business skews towards the high end" and "we believe we're insulated"; on inflation recovery, "Historically... we've put in place surcharges" and "it's tens of millions of dollars of risk, but we expect to be able to mitigate most of it over the course of the year."
  • Peter Osterland, Truist Securities, Inc., Research Division: Any demand destruction in Industrial and sustainability of Offshore growth? CEO Gliklich: "our expectation is for weaker demand growth in the Industrial Solutions business"; on Offshore, "it should be a good year... another year of high single-digit organic top line."
  • Jonathan Tanwanteng, CJS Securities, Inc.: Any electronic supply chain constraints in outlook, and EV vs. total auto expectations? CEO Gliklich: "There is risk there" but "we skew disproportionately towards the higher end"; on EV, "Our EV-exposed business has been outgrowing even EV units because we're taking share with power electronics."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts: slightly positive, with repeated validation of outperformance and durability (e.g., "Congrats on a strong Q1" and "Really nice Q1") alongside targeted risk-testing on metals, inflation, smartphones, and industrial demand.
  • Management: slightly positive in prepared remarks ("We had a great start to the year") but more cautious on macro risks in discussion ("geopolitical events have created a more complex macro environment" and "expect increased variance in quarterly earnings driven by swings in metal prices").
  • Versus last quarter, management’s language increased macro caution (Q4: "we expect market conditions to largely resemble late 2025"; Q1: "more complex macro environment") while confidence in Electronics demand remained consistent (Q4: "demand remains robust"; Q1: "Underlying demand in the high-end electronics market remains strong").

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The full-year adjusted EBITDA guide moved from "$650 million to $670 million" to "$665 million to $685 million," and Q1 added explicit commentary on "softer demand in Industrial Solutions" and a "less favorable FX tailwind." (CEO & Director Gliklich)
  • Capital intensity expectations increased as the company shifted from "For 2026, we expect capital expenditures of approximately $75 million" to "we now expect to invest between $75 million and $100 million in CapEx this year" tied to customer-led growth capacity and consolidation projects. (CFO Dorman)
  • Portfolio and integration emphasis stayed active across both calls, with Q1 updating that Micromax and EFC "are off to a solid start" and "Integration is on track," versus Q4 framing them as newly closed acquisitions expected to "flourish and grow faster and more efficiently." (CEO & Director Gliklich)

Risks and Concerns

  • "We're seeing signs of inflationary pressure" and "further supply chain disruptions and the impacts to global demand resulting from higher energy prices creates risk for our suppliers, our customers and ultimately for us." (CEO & Director Gliklich)
  • "We expect increased variance in quarterly earnings driven by swings in metal prices." (CEO & Director Gliklich)
  • "Global industrial weakness continued this quarter" and management said it remains "cautious about our European industrial demand outlook." (CFO Dorman)

Final Takeaway

Management described Q1 as a "record quarter" driven by Electronics demand tied to the "AI infrastructure build-out," alongside double-digit organic growth in Electronics and raised full-year adjusted EBITDA guidance to $665 million-$685 million. At the same time, management highlighted a more uncertain operating backdrop—citing inflation, metal-driven earnings variability, and softer Industrial demand—while increasing planned 2026 CapEx to $75 million-$100 million to support customer-led capacity additions (including Kuprion) and select consolidation projects, with a stated intent to stay "on the offensive" in high-value electronics niches.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Summers Value Fund adds TBPH, exits ADMA among Q1 moves
data.description:
The Summers Value Fund LP returned -2.7% net in Q1 2026, underperforming the Russell 2000 Index ETF, which returned 0.9%, and the Russell 2000 Value Index ETF, which returned 4.9%.

According to its quarterly fund letter, the largest contributors to the fund's performance in the first quarter included Liquidia (LQDA), Avanos Medical (AVNS), and Consensus Cloud Solutions (CCSI).

The largest detractors were Electromed (ELMD) and Embecta (EMBC).

During the quarter, the Fund initiated a new position in Theravance (TBPH) and exited ADMA Biologics (ADMA), as it fell short of the Fund’s expectations, and the Fund identified more attractive opportunities for its capital.

The Fund also strengthened its position in Embecta (EMBC) following a sell-off after the company narrowed its fiscal 2026 guidance to the low end of the range.

Source: Q1 fund letter
